summary: Expanding multiplies everything out, turning products and powers of brackets into a sum of plain terms. It is the opposite of factoring.
method: Apply the distributive law: multiply each term in one bracket by each term in the other.
Use the known identities for squares and differences of squares to speed things up.
Multiply out any powers of brackets, e.g. (x + 1)² = (x + 1)(x + 1).
Collect like terms to tidy the result into a single polynomial.

example.title: Expand a squared binomial
steps: Multiply each pair: x·x + x·3 + 3·x + 3·3 = x² + 3x + 3x + 9.
